sutta: 2 1 on 1 occasion when he was dwelling when he was staying near basali at the gabled hall in mahavana the exalted 1 rising from seclusion in the evening visited the hall of the sikh and the exalted 1 saw a monk weak and ailing and at the side he sat down on a seat made ready and when he was seated the exalted 1 addressed the monk saying monks if 5 things forsake not anyone weak and ailing for him this may be expected before long by destroying the asavas he will enter and abide in the emancipation by mine the emancipation by wisdom which is free of the asabas realizing this by his own knowledge even here and now what 5 here in monks among the bites seeing the unattractiveness of the body he is conscious of the session applying of food conscious of distaste as to the world perceives impermanence in the conditioned and his inner self is well set on the thought of death monks if these 5 things forsake not anyone weak and ailing for him this may be expected before long he will enter and abide in emancipation so this is the end of the sultan this is talking about the 5 contemplations that is very helpful for a person to obtain liberation even when a person is very sick and weak and ailing if he practices these 5 contemplations intensely then he can obtain liberation the first 1 is contemplating the unattractiveness of the body sometimes it's translated as the lonesomeness of the body the second 1 is the conscious of the satiation of food not the safety of of uh food being tired of food and like a feeling of too much food second 1 conscious of the distaste as to the world to to be able to see and that this the world is not so attractive as it seems then impermanence seeing impermanence in the condition all conditioned things i mean all things in the world are impermanent and keep on changing and last 1 the inner self is well set on the thought of death that means uh you can see here that you contemplate that always contemplating death so these are the few uh contemplations which are very important the unattractiveness of the body the satiation of food a clawing of food distaste of the world impermanence of conditioned things that means all things in the world and constant contemplation of death the next utah is
